title: Filter and vacuum food processor
abstract: Embodiments of present disclosure relates to a filter for use in a vacuum food processor and a vacuum food processor including the filter. The filter comprising: a first filter part adapted to direct a suction airflow generated by a vacuum module of the vacuum food processor into the filter via an inlet channel; and a second filter part adapted to coaxially fit with the first filter part and comprising a cavity, the cavity adapted to maintain non-gaseous substance that is moving along with the suction airflow inside the filter, wherein the first filter part and the second filter part jointly define an outlet channel at a bottom of first filter part, the outlet channel adapted to direct the suction airflow outside the filter. In this way, the food particles/bubbles can be prevented from entering the vacuum module, while the suction airflow is allowed to freely flow to the vacuum module.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. A filter (10) for use in a vacuum food processor, characterized in comprising: a first filter part (1) adapted to direct a suction airflow (20) generated by a vacuum module of the vacuum food processor into the filter (10) via an inlet channel (105); and a second filter part (2) adapted to coaxially fit with the first filter part (1) and comprising a cavity (4), an inner surface of the cavity (4) adapted to maintain non-gaseous substance that is moving along with the suction airflow (20) inside the filter (10), wherein the first filter part (1) and the second filter part (2) jointly define an outlet channel (3) at a bottom (106) of first filter part (1), the outlet channel (3) adapted to direct the suction airflow (20) outside the filter (10). 2. The filter (10) of claim 1, characterized in that, the first filter part (1) comprises a first rib (101) extending in a circumferential direction (C) and oriented in a axial direction (Y) of the filter (10); and the second filter part (2) comprises a second rib (202) extending in the circumferential direction (C) and oriented in the axial direction (Y), wherein the first rib (101) and the second rib (202), when fitted with each other, form a first gap (31) as a first part of the outlet channel (3). 3. The filter (10) of claim 2, characterized in that, when the first rib (101) and the second rib (202) are fitted with each other, the first rib (101) is separated from the second rib (202) by a first offset in a radial direction (R), and/or by a second offset in the axial direction (Y). 4. The filter (10) of claim 2 or 3, characterized in that, the first filter part (1) further comprises a third rib (103) oriented in the axial direction (Y) and coaxially arranged with the first rib (101), the third rib (3) being provided outside the first rib (101) in a radial direction (R); and 

 the second filter part (2) further comprises a fourth rib (204) oriented in the axial direction (Y) and coaxially arranged with the second rib (202), the fourth rib (204) being provided outside the second rib (202) in the radial direction (R), wherein when the second filter part (2) is fit with the first filter part (1), the third rib (103) and the fourth rib (204) forms a second gap (32) as a second part of the outlet channel (3)). 5. The filter (10) of claim 4, characterized in that, when the third rib (103) and the fourth rib (204) are fitted with each other, the third rib (103) is separated from the fourth rib (204) by a third offset in a radial direction (R), and/or by a fourth offset in the axial direction (Y). 6. The filter (10) of claim 4 or 5, characterized in that, when the second filter part (2) is fit with the first filter part (1), the second rib (202) is arranged downstream to the first rib (101) along a direction of the suction airflow (20), the third rib (103) is arranged downstream to the second rib (202) along the direction of the suction airflow (20), and the fourth rib (204) is arranged downstream to the third rib (103) along the direction of the suction airflow (20). 7. The filter (10) according to any preceding claim, characterized in that the first filter part (1) comprises a first rib (101), the first rib (1) extending in a circumferential direction (C) and having a first end face perpendicular to the axial direction (Y) of the filter (10); and the second filter part (2) comprises a second end face perpendicular to the axial direction (Y), wherein when the first filter element (1) is fitted with the second filter element (2), the first end face is in contact with the second end face in the axial direction (Y), but with a micro gap therebetween for the suction airflow (20) to pass through, and the micro gap forms a part of the outlet channel (3). 8. The filter (10) of any preceding claim, characterized in that the cavity (4) comprises a first sub-cavity (41) and a second sub-cavity (42) surrounding the first sub-cavity (41) in a circumferential direction (C), 

 wherein the first sub-cavity (41) defines a gripping region (205) on an outer surface (200) of the second filter part (2), and the second sub-cavity (42) defines an inclined region (206) on the outer surface of the second filter part (2). 9. The filter (10) of any preceding claim, characterized in that the first filter part (1) comprises a fastening hook (107); and the second filter part (2) comprises a fastening hole (208) adapted to receive the fastening hook (107) to fit the second filter part (2) with the first filter part (1). 10. The filter (10) of any preceding claim, characterized in that the first filter part (1) comprises a connecting part (108) adapted to detachably hinge the first filter part (1) to a lid of the vacuum food processor in a radial direction (R). 11. The filter (10) of any preceding claim, characterized in that the first filter part (1) comprises a pin (109), and the second filter part (2) comprises a slot (209) adapted to receive the pin (109) to prevent a rotation of the second filter part (2) with respect to the fist filter part (1) in a circumferential direction (C). 12. The filter (10) of anyone of claims 2 to 11, characterized in that the first rib (101) is adapted to support the second filter part (2) when the second filter part (2) is fit with the first filter part (1), wherein the first rib (101) comprises a contact surface facing toward the inner surface of the cavity (4), the contact surface is adapted to match an inner surface of the inclined region (206). 13. The filter (10) of anyone of claims 4 to 12, characterized in that the first rib (101) is integrally formed with the first filter part (1), and the second rib (202) is integrally formed with the second filter part (2); and/or the third rib (103) is integrally formed with the first filter part (1), and the fourth rib (204) is integrally formed with the second filter part (2). 14. The filter (10) of any preceding claim, characterized in that the first filter part (1) comprises a handle (104). 15. A vacuum food processor, characterized in comprising the filter (10) according to any of claims 1-14.
